title: Emergency Medical Technicians
description: Assess injuries and illnesses and administer basic emergency medical care. May transport injured or sick persons to medical facilities.

tasks.text: Administer first aid treatment or life support care to sick or injured persons in prehospital settings.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assess nature and extent of illness or injury to establish and prioritize medical procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Attend training classes to maintain certification licensure, keep abreast of new developments in the field, or maintain existing knowledge.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Communicate with dispatchers or treatment center personnel to provide information about situation, to arrange reception of survivors, or to receive instructions for further treatment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Coordinate work with other emergency medical team members or police or fire department personnel.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Decontaminate ambulance interior following treatment of patient with infectious disease, and report case to proper authorities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Drive mobile intensive care unit to specified location, following instructions from emergency medical dispatcher.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Immobilize patient for placement on stretcher and ambulance transport, using backboard or other spinal immobilization device.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain vehicles and medical and communication equipment, and replenish first aid equipment and supplies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe, record, and report to physician the patient's condition or injury, the treatment provided, and reactions to drugs or treatment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform emergency diagnostic and treatment procedures, such as stomach suction, airway management, or heart monitoring, during ambulance ride.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
